Title: Identification and reconstitution of the rubber biosynthetic machinery on rubber particles from Hevea brasiliensis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances HRT1-dependent rubber transferase activity and bridges HRT1 with REF to support natural rubber biosynthetic machinery on rubber particles.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HRBP, a Nogo-B receptor homologue, was identified on detergent-washed rubber particles and is predominantly expressed in latex. 2. HRBP interacts with HRT1 and REF, mediating formation of an HRT1-HRBP-REF ternary complex. 3. HRBP promotes HRT1 incorporation/stabilization on washed rubber particles and enhances HRT1-derived rubber transferase activity, although HRBP alone does not activate the background activity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in latex; much lower in leaves, stems, roots, and suspension-cultured cells.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NgBR/NUS1 family protein; Nogo-B receptor homologue with predicted N-terminal transmembrane domain
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HRT1; REF; HRBP; HRT2 (BiFC only, not supported by Y2H)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RP proteomics; quantitative RT-PCR; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; bimolecular fluorescence complementation; co-expression/subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; co-immunoprecipitation; cell-free translation-coupled introduction onto washed rubber particles; RTase assay with 14C-IPP
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; small dots/particles; relocates to Golgi with HRT1 and to plasma membrane with HRT2; in ternary complex with HRT1 and REF mainly on ER
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: HRBP expression on washed rubber particles promoted coagulation/aggregation unless REF was co-expressed; no direct independent RTase activation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful partner gene for reconstituting or enhancing rubber biosynthesis when stacked with HRT1 and REF; may improve correct membrane targeting/stabilization of cis-prenyltransferase in metabolic engineering.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (LC-MS/MS on detergent-washed rubber particles); HRBP mRNA accession LC057267; Hevea transcriptome reference PRJNA82895
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Detergent washing of rubber particles with CHAPS; cell-free translation-coupled introduction onto washed rubber particles
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as HRT1-REF bridging protein rather than a catalytic enzyme; NgBR homolog involvement appears supportive for efficient RTase assembly/stability, but not absolutely required for cPT catalytic activity on WRPs.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stabilizes washed rubber particles, prevents aggregation, and strongly enhances HRT1-HRBP-dependent rubber transferase activity as a component of the ternary complex.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. REF physically interacts with HRBP, and REF co-expression redirects the HRT1-HRBP complex from Golgi to the ER. 2. REF is co-immunoprecipitated with HRBP and HRT1/HRT2, supporting ternary complex formation on rubber particles. 3. REF alone does not activate RTase, but markedly boosts HRT1 activity together with HRBP and stabilizes washed rubber particles by reducing coagulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly abundant in latex according to cited and proteomic context.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HRBP; REF; SRPP; HRT1/HRT2 indirectly in ternary complex
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Proteomics context; split-ubiquitin yeast two-hybrid; bimolecular fluorescence complementation; co-expression/subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; co-immunoprecipitation; dynamic light scattering of WRPs; cell-free reconstitution and RTase assay
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum; specific particles/aggregates on rubber particle-related structures
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct RTase activity alone; functional effect depends on co-expression with HRT1 and HRBP.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important accessory gene to co-express with HRT1 and HRBP for efficient and stable rubber biosynthesis and particle stability in engineering systems.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CHAPS washing of rubber particles; in vitro translation-coupled reconstitution on WRPs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although historically implicated in rubber biosynthesis, this study provides direct functional evidence that REF is a module of the RTase machinery and a rubber particle stabilizer.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Distinctly increases rubber transferase activity and supports de novo natural rubber biosynthesis on washed rubber particles; activity is enhanced by HRBP and further by REF.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: IPP plus allylic diphosphate primer substrates, especially FPP; also GPP and GGPP
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially condenses IPP units onto allylic diphosphate primers to form high-molecular-weight polyisoprenoid/natural rubber on rubber particles.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed polymerization step / terminal product-forming step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as principal catalytic component of RTase machinery; key control step for rubber formation on WRPs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HRT1 exhibits distinct rubber transferase activity only when introduced onto detergent-washed rubber particles, not on liposomes. 2. HRT1 activity is enhanced by HRBP and markedly stimulated by additional REF, indicating function within a protein complex. 3. HRT1-generated products have size distributions similar to endogenous natural rubber, supporting HRT1 as the principal catalytic component of rubber biosynthesis in Hevea.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in latex according to cited prior work and discussion.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi and weakly ER when co-expressed with HRBP; shifts to ER with HRBP plus REF; on washed rubber particles in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cell-free translation-coupled introduction onto washed rubber particles; RTase assay using 14C-IPP and allylic substrates; GPC product profiling; co-expression with HRBP/REF; BiFC and co-IP interaction evidence
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires specific rubber particle membrane architecture for activity; HRT1 expression on WRPs promotes coagulation unless REF is present.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primary enzyme target for engineering enhanced natural rubber production, ideally stacked with HRBP and REF and targeted to authentic rubber particle-like membrane architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: CHAPS-washed rubber particles; wheat germ cell-free expression; allylic primer availability (FPP preferred, GPP and GGPP also active)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics detected HRT1/HRT2 peptides on detergent-washed rubber particles but could not discriminate between them
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study argues HRT1 is more important than HRT2 for NR biosynthesis because of localization and reconstitution results, although HRT2 involvement is not completely excluded.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: When introduced onto Hevea washed rubber particles, heterologous LsCPT3 confers distinct rubber transferase activity and produces high-molecular-weight rubber-like polyisoprenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hevea latex rubber particles in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: IPP plus allylic diphosphate primer (assayed with FPP)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-prenyl chain elongation from IPP and allylic primer when properly introduced onto Hevea rubber particles.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Heterologous lettuce LsCPT3 reconstituted on Hevea WRPs exhibits clear RTase activity. 2. Product size distribution resembles that produced by HRT1 and endogenous Hevea rubber. 3. Results support the idea that proper membrane architecture of WRPs is critical for RTase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in lettuce latex according to paper context/cited work.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: On Hevea washed rubber particles in vitro after cell-free introduction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cell-free translation-coupled protein introduction onto Hevea WRPs; RTase assay with 14C-IPP and FPP; GPC analysis of products
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires Hevea WRP architecture for activity; no evidence of independent function on artificial liposomes in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates cross-species enzyme deployment for rubber biosynthesis, suggesting heterologous cPTs can be engineered if correctly targeted to rubber-particle architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hevea WRP-based reconstitution system; FPP supplied as allylic primer
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in Hevea WRPs as applied species; source species and applied species should be distinguished.
